title: The Man In The Blue Flannel Pants

jokes.text: Is there any better feeling than waiting for a live Krusty show to start? Maybe watching your bride coming down the aisle. Hmm.
jokes.setup: Character asking about the best feeling ever
jokes.punchline: Bart considers wedding day only 'maybe' better than waiting for a clown show
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Bart's childish priorities, well-structured with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Why does she get to eat before the reception?
jokes.punchline: Child's literal interpretation of bride eating at wedding
jokes.explanation: Solid child logic humor but fairly predictable

jokes.text: My comedy comes from taking risks. Or avoiding them, I can't remember.
jokes.punchline: Complete contradiction and admission of not remembering his own approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character beat - shows his incompetence and self-awareness in equal measure

jokes.text: Oh, why can't I be funny with just my words?
jokes.punchline: Krusty lamenting inability to be verbally funny while being verbally funny
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about comedy writing itself - ironic because the line is funny

jokes.text: Bill Maher doesn't put dangerous things near his crotch, except when he's off work.
jokes.setup: Comparing Krusty's dangerous physical comedy to other comedians
jokes.punchline: Implication about Bill Maher's personal life
jokes.explanation: Cheap celebrity reference joke without much craft

jokes.text: Always gets me.
jokes.punchline: Krusty falls for his own product gimmick every time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's childlike nature

jokes.text: I used up my stuff poisoning deer.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he drank competitor's vodka
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of using his vodka to poison animals
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Simpsons humor - casual delivery of something horrific

jokes.text: Get to the point! Unless you want a face full of clown chunks!
jokes.punchline: Threatening someone with 'clown chunks' - absurd threat
jokes.explanation: Good absurd threat that fits Krusty's character

jokes.text: We got buzz. Positive buzz. That exists? Wow.
jokes.setup: Agent explaining they need positive buzz
jokes.punchline: Krusty's genuine surprise that positive buzz exists
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's low expectations

jokes.text: I need a drink. Not that! Never that!
jokes.setup: Agent needs a drink after stressful conversation
jokes.punchline: Rejects the vodka they're trying to market
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective irony about their own product

jokes.text: Children of troubled marriages not eligible. Oh!
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's immediate disappointed reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - his parents' divorce is running gag

jokes.text: So, uh, why are you here?
jokes.punchline: Bart's confusion about why Milhouse is still there
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to previous beat, functional

jokes.text: Krappy the Klunk. See?
jokes.punchline: Name 'Krappy the Klunk' as obvious Krusty ripoff
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly obvious joke

jokes.text: the most redonkulous marketing outreach
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'redonkulous'
jokes.explanation: Generic made-up word, not particularly clever

jokes.text: He can listen to music with headphones on, he gets one slice of cheese pizza, and if the party gets slow, he has to pretend to let Krusty save him from drowning.
jokes.setup: Describing Bart's one friend's party privileges
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd restrictions ending with fake drowning
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with perfect specific details

jokes.text: Redonkulous! Off the dilge!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to use cool slang
jokes.punchline: Making up nonsense words like 'dilge'
jokes.explanation: Homer misusing slang fits character, callback to agent's word

jokes.text: And that's why John Gielgud is more of a father to me than my own father.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy mid-conversation at party
jokes.punchline: Claiming actor John Gielgud as father figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy pretentiousness with obscure actor reference

jokes.text: There'll be time enough for nachos!
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's dramatic dismissal of nacho concerns
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Comic Book Guy's dramatic nature

jokes.text: Yeah, even Homer's not dumb enough to invite... So! This is the famous party. Feh. Guh. Pffoy.
jokes.setup: Characters saying Homer wouldn't invite Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately appears with disgusted sounds
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup with perfect timing

jokes.text: He's destroying our youthful demographic!
jokes.punchline: Agent's panic about demographic destruction
jokes.explanation: Functional joke about Burns' age but not particularly clever

jokes.text: So the car in front of us forgot to validate his... uh... I was in an anecdote once. Um, I have surgery in the morning. Yeah, and I'm the one having the surgery.
jokes.setup: Party guests trying to avoid Burns
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and nonsensical excuses to leave
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity of excuse-making

jokes.text: Your cotillion seems to be in full promenade. Good one.
jokes.setup: Burns making old-fashioned observation about party
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused attempt to agree
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Burns' vocabulary and Homer's confusion

jokes.text: Do you have anything by Prince... Wilhelm of Prussia?
jokes.setup: Burns asked about popular songs, starts to say Prince
jokes.punchline: Reveals he means 19th century Prussian royalty
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke perfectly suited to Burns' ancient character

jokes.text: Just play the oldest, stupidest thing in your catalog. Uh, let me check my beeswax cylinders.
jokes.punchline: DJ having to check beeswax cylinders (pre-record technology)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Burns' ancient tastes affecting modern DJ

jokes.text: May you all fly in an aeroplane someday.
jokes.punchline: Treating air travel as futuristic blessing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment showing his disconnect from modern times

jokes.text: Indeed. That Homer Simpson is the beating baboon heart of this party.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor comparing Homer to baboon heart
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Burns metaphor that's both complimentary and insulting

jokes.text: So he says, 'Bigamy is having one too many wives.' To which I replied, 'So's monogamy.'
jokes.setup: Burns telling old joke about bigamy
jokes.punchline: Clever twist that monogamy is also 'one too many'
jokes.explanation: Classic well-structured joke with perfect wordplay and cynical Burns worldview

jokes.text: It's never good. I always wind up being chased by hounds, or falling through a trapdoor, or being a peg in your game of human croquet.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd punishments he's endured
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's history with Burns' schemes

jokes.text: When I made you a hoop, you split your pants.
jokes.punchline: Burns' specific memory of Homer's failure as croquet hoop
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: The last thing I understood in that was 'pants.'
jokes.punchline: Homer only understanding the word 'pants'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity moment with great economy

jokes.text: In two weeks, the only tie I'll be wearing is for autoerotic pleasure.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual reference in workplace
jokes.explanation: Shocking overshare that establishes Marlowe's character

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! No more church!
jokes.setup: Burns warns about losing soul in new job
jokes.punchline: Homer excited about not having to go to church
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic missing the point completely

jokes.text: The higher they rise, the further they fall! You know, you're kind of a downer.
jokes.setup: Lenny giving ominous warning about success
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt assessment of Lenny's negativity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of dramatic moment

jokes.text: Hey, you're a baby account man. Still on the bottle.
jokes.punchline: Pun about baby/bottle relating to drinking
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that establishes business drinking culture

jokes.text: Aw. Bourbon go bye-bye. There ain't enough bourbon in Kentucky for you, big guy. Yeah, the governor wrote me a letter to that effect.
jokes.punchline: Marlowe claiming governor banned him from Kentucky bourbon
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with absurd specific detail about governor

jokes.text: The next time, I vote for you, it's gonna be on purpose.
jokes.punchline: Admission he's voted for mayor accidentally
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment about his voting incompetence

jokes.text: And if you don't like the score, you can change it yourself. ♪ Boring. ♪
jokes.setup: Homer showing off luxury box features
jokes.punchline: Homer changing scoreboard to say 'Boring'
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing Homer's childish use of power

jokes.text: I have never heard an analogy involving sports! I love it!
jokes.punchline: Quimby's excitement over basic sports metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Quimby's political bubble

jokes.text: This set of bar tools has been passed down from account man to account man since the dawn of history. 1956.
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'dawn of history' was 1956
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of pompous tradition with specific date

jokes.text: Can you be my dad? If your mom was a secretary, there's a good chance I am.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Marlowe to be father figure
jokes.punchline: Marlowe's casual admission of workplace affairs
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace culture and Marlowe's past

jokes.text: Can you be my dad? If your mom was a secretary, there's a good chance I am.
jokes.punchline: Marlowe's casual admission of workplace affairs
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace relationships delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: It's a rat race, but it looks like I won.
jokes.punchline: Homer's misunderstanding of 'rat race' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer misunderstanding of common expression

jokes.text: Read to Lisa? Why don't you just ask me to kiss her? If you kissed her, that would be lovely. I'll read! I'll read!
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about reading to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's suggestion making kissing sound appealing compared to reading
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with escalating grossness

jokes.text: Please, Lisa. I had 18 martinis today.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission of extreme drinking
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about business drinking culture affecting family

jokes.text: I'm a bad reader, okay?!
jokes.punchline: Bart's frustrated admission of illiteracy
jokes.explanation: Good character vulnerability moment for Bart

jokes.text: I'll get one of those jobs where you don't need to read, like French fry maker or general.
jokes.setup: Bart saying he doesn't need to read
jokes.punchline: Pairing minimum wage job with military leadership
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of lowly and high-ranking jobs

jokes.text: Don't you want to be able to read the things people carve into your chest in prison?
jokes.punchline: Suggesting prison chest carving as reading motivation
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully dark Lisa logic with shocking imagery

jokes.text: No, Bart, you can read other books, too. What?!
jokes.punchline: Bart's shock that reading skills transfer to other books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart stupidity about how reading works

jokes.text: You can't touch electricity, Marge, you can't feel it. That's because it would kill you.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting meaningless work with electricity
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical response about electrical safety
jokes.explanation: Good Marge as voice of reason deflating Homer's melodrama

jokes.text: My job is my wife, loneliness-- my mistress, despair-- my sex buddy. Angst is a chick I met online, but then it turned out it was really a guy.
jokes.setup: Homer making dramatic metaphors about his emotional state
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd online dating story about angst
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating metaphor ending in absurd catfishing story

jokes.text: Angst is a chick I met online, but then it turned out it was really a guy.
jokes.punchline: Taking the metaphor to literal online dating territory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant continuation of metaphor into absurd literal territory

jokes.text: Sorry, Marge, that account job hollowed me out like a cheap chocolate bunny.
jokes.punchline: Specific metaphor about hollow chocolate bunnies
jokes.explanation: Good specific metaphor that's both relatable and character-appropriate

jokes.text: I've been divorced three times, I've got a cocktail addiction, a shrimp cocktail addiction, and I can't stop bending women to my sexual will.
jokes.punchline: Escalating list including shrimp cocktail addiction and inappropriate claim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with wordplay on cocktail types

jokes.text: Wait, how did I end up in your bedroom? Lady, I am smoother than an Eisenhower-era freeway.
jokes.punchline: Marlowe's dated smoothness comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with era-appropriate metaphor

jokes.text: Then I gave up on my secret other family.
jokes.setup: Marlowe talking about giving up on family
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of secret second family
jokes.explanation: Good casual reveal of additional shocking information

jokes.text: It's not what you think. It's much weirder.
jokes.setup: Luann walking in on suspicious situation
jokes.punchline: Promise that the truth is stranger than assumption
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that raises more questions than it answers

jokes.text: You're reading a chapter book. For fun! Bullies, to me.
jokes.punchline: Nelson summoning other bullies like calling reinforcements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment treating reading as serious offense

jokes.text: Little Women? Are you aware that's another way of saying 'girls'?
jokes.punchline: Bully's literal interpretation of classic title
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing bully's literal thinking

jokes.text: Your fists are sisters? Yeah, Poke-ahontas and Sock-ajawea!
jokes.punchline: Jimbo's puns on Native American names for fighting
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining historical figures with fighting terms

jokes.text: You can't stop now. I want to know if their father comes home. Does Jo learn to handle her hasty temper? Does Amy get over her conceitedness?
jokes.setup: Bullies forced to listen to Little Women
jokes.punchline: Bullies genuinely invested in character development
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal of tough guys caring about literature

jokes.text: 'Cause we're going to keep making you read us a lame ol' girl's book.
jokes.punchline: Punishment is continuing to read book they clearly enjoy
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining irony of tough guys' literary interest

jokes.text: I keep going, 'Why is Meg saying that?' and then I realize it's Beth.
jokes.punchline: Bully confused by character differentiation in classic novel
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail about bully engaging seriously with literature

jokes.text: Tell her I'm at a meeting, not drinking. This is a whisper-amplifying intercom.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to hide drinking from Marge
jokes.punchline: Secretary revealing the intercom broadcasts everything
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with technology undermining Homer's plan

jokes.text: Don't talk to me like I'm a client. You're not a client, you're the client.
jokes.punchline: Homer doubling down with worse business speak
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's business persona overtaking family life

jokes.text: Finally, humanity has found a use for water.
jokes.punchline: Burns treating water recreation as humanity's first use for water
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line showing his disconnect from basic human needs

jokes.text: Coggily-doggily.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to think through his scheduling conflict
jokes.punchline: Made-up exclamation suggesting thinking
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer nonsense exclamation suggesting mental activity

jokes.text: It's more fun to play with a ball of yarn if it has all the colors in it. And yarn should be allowed to marry dental floss.
jokes.setup: Lisa presumably acting strange from context
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical statements about yarn and dental floss marriage
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist humor that doesn't quite land without visual context

jokes.text: Let's see, my family gives me love and sex. My work gives me money and sleep.
jokes.setup: Homer weighing family vs work priorities
jokes.punchline: Reducing both to basic transactional elements
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic reducing complex relationships to simple trades

jokes.text: How ironic. I survived the Titanic by making a raft out of steerage passengers, and now this.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of using people as flotation devices on Titanic
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Burns humor with historical reference and casual evil

jokes.text: Mr. Burns, your heart will go on.
jokes.punchline: Homer's reference to Celine Dion Titanic theme song
jokes.explanation: Weak pop culture reference that doesn't add much to the scene

jokes.text: I'm okay, and I found some gold! See, luckiest man in the world.
jokes.setup: Homer falling into water after dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Homer finding positive in disaster and claiming luck
jokes.explanation: Good Homer optimism in the face of disaster

jokes.text: I'm back to being a boring old safety inspector at a nuclear power plant.
jokes.setup: Homer reassuring Marge about returning to normal job
jokes.punchline: Calling nuclear safety inspector job 'boring' when it's incredibly dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending showing Homer's obliviousness to his job's importance

tmdb_overview: The Simpsons host a party to promote Krusty's own brand of liquor; impressed with the party's sucess, Mr. Burns decides to promote Homer; Bart takes an interest in reading classic novels.
